channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US Embassy issues warning to Americans in Trinidad and Tobago as tensions with Venezuela escalate
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago is cautioning Americans to stay away from U.S. government facilities on the twin-island nation.
item.dc:creator: Anselm Gibbs, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Afghan and Pakistani delegations in Doha for crisis talks over cross-border violence
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghan and Pakistani delegations were in the Qatari capital, Doha, on Saturday, for talks on defusing the deadliest crisis between them in several years, after more than a week of fighting killed dozens of people and injured hundreds on both sides.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Andrew's antics and scandals have tried royal patience, triggering headlines and suspicions
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew was forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 76 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drone bombing of police building not a ‘declaration of war’ from cartels, expert says
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Thursday’s brazen drone bombing of a police building in Tijuana, Mexico, likely was meant as a warning and not a declaration of war from a criminal organization, a private security expert says.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy pitches Trump on Ukraine drone-for-Tomahawk missile exchange as president weighs escalation concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump shows interest in a potential drone deal with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy but calls Tomahawk missiles a significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says it is not 'easy' for US to give Tomahawk missiles to Zelensky, Ukraine
item.description: President Trump during a White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said it is not “easy” for the U.S. to give long-range Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine, as Washington needs the powerful munitions in its stockpile also.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polish court blocks extradition and frees Ukrainian suspected in Nord Stream pipeline blasts
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A Polish court on Friday blocked the extradition to Germany of a Ukrainian man suspected of involvement in the 2022 attack on the Nord Stream gas pipelines and ordered his release, a ruling that was welcomed by Poland's prime minister.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA CIOBANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran’s execution rate tops 1,000 this year as death row inmates launch hunger strike
item.description: Iranian death row prisoners call on U.N. Secretary General António Guterres to intervene as the regime executes over 1,000 people since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King to pray with pope in first since 1534
item.description: VATICAN CITY/LONDON, Oct 17 (Reuters) - Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican next week in a visit seen as a sign of closening ties between the Catholic Church and the Anglican Communion, five centuries after their turbulent separation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua McElwee and Muvija M, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Trump explains father's hot mic moment with Indonesian president
item.description: Eric Trump sought to clarify Thursday what President Trump and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto talked about during a hot mic moment when the commander in chief's son came up during a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Hannibal Gadhafi, son of late Libyan leader, ordered released in Lebanon if he pays $11 million bail
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A Lebanese judge on Friday ordered the release of the son of Libya’s late leader Moammar Gadhafi on condition that he pay $11 million bail. Hannibal Gadhafi has been imprisoned in Lebanon for a decade without being charged.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky meets with Energy secretary, US companies ahead of visit with Trump
item.description: Ahead of his upcoming meeting with President Trump on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he met with Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright and various American energy and defense companies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Trump expresses optimism for Ukraine deal as Zelenskyy arrives in DC
item.description: “We need Tomahawks for the United States of America too,” Trump said. “We have a lot of them, but we need them. I mean, we can’t deplete our country.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Political crisis in France eases for now as prime minister survives no-confidence vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s latest political crisis eased — for now — when Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu survived two consecutive no-confidence votes on Thursday, averting another government collapse and giving President Emmanuel Macron a respite before an even tougher fight over the national budget.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at the hostages whose remains are still inside Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas freed the last 20 living hostages taken during its Oct. 7, 2023, attack on southern Israel in exchange for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Putin plan Hungary meeting on Ukraine war, Zelenskyy to visit White House Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump schedules call with Russian President Vladimir Putin as Ukraine seeks long-range Tomahawk missiles following recent Russian strikes on energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia makes more precise drones and is using them to attack Ukraine’s vital rail network
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Russian drones smashed into the Shostka train station in northeastern Ukraine earlier this month, they killed a 71-year-old man, injured at least eight people and left train cars buckled by fire and riddled with shrapnel holes.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American who was on death row in China details horrifying experiences
item.description: Speaking in his first television interview since being released in November 2024 as part of a prisoner swap, Swidan detailed his experience on "Elizabeth Vargas Reports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli hostages returning home need space to adjust, father says
item.description: "People need to realize that the hostages that we know so well for two years are not a public property," says Adi Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners freed as Trump tours region
item.description: Hamas released all 20 of the last living hostages on Monday, the Israeli military said, as part of a breakthrough ceasefire after two years of war between Israel and Hamas in the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza photojournalist says his disabled brother is missing
item.description: A Gaza-based freelance photojournalist said his disabled brother went missing in August after seeking aid. The family hoped he would be returned today.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: Israeli, Palestinian families celebrate return of hostages, prisoners
item.description: The last remaining living Israeli hostages were released as well as more than 1,900 detained Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Russia he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawks if Moscow doesn't settle war soon
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump on Sunday warned Russia that he may send Ukraine long-range Tomahawk missiles if Moscow doesn’t settle its war there soon — suggesting that he could be ready to increase the pressure on Vladimir Putin's government using a key weapons system.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9
